label: Neuro-symbolic models

node.label: Neuro-symbolic models
node.definition: A neuro-symbolic model combines learned continuous representations with symbolic rules, programs, constraints, knowledge graphs, or proof procedures.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Neuro-symbolic models is to connect Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution to downstream capabilities including Theorem proving & symbolic deduction, Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI.
examples: substrate for Theorem proving & symbolic deduction
substrate for Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.

dependencies.rationale: Neuro-symbolic models follows the learning setup expressed by Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning.
Neuro-symbolic models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
Neuro-symbolic models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.
Neuro-symbolic models extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
